reasoning: The base rate for 'Explosions/Remote violence' in Burundi over the past year is approx 0.333 per 30 days, which indicates an event occurs somewhat rarely (approx 4 times a year). Extensive English and French web searches returned no reported grenade attacks, bombings, or remote violence events in Burundi during the elapsed 23 days of this window. Given a ~7.5% chance of an event in the remaining 7 days, plus a ~5% chance that a recent event was missed by web coverage (since ACLED draws heavily on local trackers), a 0.12 estimate is appropriate. For the second resolution date (Mar 17, 2026), the window is effectively the next 30 days. Without any detected spike in recent tension, reverting to the baseline historical probability of ~28% (calculated via Poisson distribution 1-e^-0.333) is the most robust estimation.
